tossups.question: A product of this name that was designed "to become an omnimedium" was publicly demonstrated with both Amelia Earhart and Eleanor Roosevelt before the company went bankrupt. A scheme to take half-hour naps once every six hours was dubbed this kind of "sleep." This is the first word in the name of a map profiled in Life magazine that projected onto an icosahedron to avoid some of the distortions in a Mercator projection. Alec Nevala-Lee documented the numerous fatal accidents of a car of this name that had only a single rear wheel. An inventor had to throw pebbles at kids to keep them from climbing onto the roof of a structure of this name in Carbondale, Illinois. That house of this name had (*) hexagonal walls making it hard to hang pictures. For 10 points, name this word used in a number of products dreamed up by Buckminster Fuller, which combines "dynamic" with two other words.
tossups.answer: Dymaxion [accept Dymaxion car or Dymaxion house or Dymaxion map or Dymaxion sleep]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Dymaxion [accept Dymaxion car or Dymaxion house or Dymaxion map or Dymaxion sleep]
